item.title: Power Firms Jump on Data-Center Timeline From Biggest US Grid
item.description: Shares of power-plant owners climbed after the largest US electric grid accelerated plans to pair data centers with energy producers.

item.title: BOE Governor Bailey Says Food Price Controls ‘Not Sustainable’
item.description: Bank of England Governor Andrew Bailey said government controls on food prices are not sustainable in the long run, after reports that the UK Treasury asked supermarkets to cap the cost of essential products.

item.title: The Bond Market Sees AI Making Warsh’s Inflation Bind Even Worse
item.description: To judge by a key bond-market signal, the AI boom is only worsening Kevin Warsh’s inflation problem.

item.title: US Crude Inventories Drop By Most on Record as Exports Surge
item.description: US crude inventories, including strategic reserves, plunged by a record 17.8 million barrels last week as record exports start to erode the domestic supply cushions.

item.title: Chile's Big Economic Bill Clears Another Hurdle as Kast Seeks to Drive Growth
item.description: Chile’s lower house of Congress approved the base text of President José Antonio Kast’s flagship economic bill that his administration is counting on to drive growth in coming years.

item.title: Security Firm Thwarting Nation-State Hackers Valued at $1 Billion
item.description: Socket, a cybersecurity startup that sells technology to help safeguard open-source code against hackers, has raised a new round of funding that values the company at $1 billion.

item.title: Samsung Reaches Tentative Union Deal, Averting Crippling Strike
item.description: Samsung Electronics Co. reached a tentative deal with its labor union, averting what had threatened to be a crippling strike at the world’s largest memory chipmaker.

item.title: Solar Eclipse, Cod Catch and Aluminum Factor in Icelandic Rates
item.description: Setting interest rates for a land of fewer than 400,000 people can have a central bank considering the monetary-policy impact of unexpected factors from the movements of celestial bodies to catches of fish.

item.title: Austria Urges Faster EU-UAE Trade Talks After Plastics Mega-Deal
item.description: Austria’s economy minister urged the European Union to accelerate free-trade talks with the United Arab Emirates, after state-owned chemical companies in both nations recently completed a merger worth more than $60 billion.

item.title: Can Venezuela Rebuild Investor Trust With a Debt Overhaul?
item.description: Venezuela is seeking to kick off one of the biggest debt restructurings in modern financial history, as the government attempts to revive the economy after years of turmoil. If successful, the renegotiation of the terms of $170 billion of bonds, loans and other claims that are in default could reopen the country’s access to international markets and fresh investment.

item.title: Switzerland Wants Workers to Retire Later But Won’t Force Them
item.description: Switzerland’s government is trying to shore up the state pension system without explicitly requiring people to stay in the workforce for longer.

item.title: Cash Crunch Leaves Braskem’s Mexico Unit Missing Out on Oil Boom
item.description: Braskem SA’s beleaguered petrochemical Mexico unit is missing out on the energy prices boom, hobbled by a cash crunch even as investors push its bonds higher.

item.title: Commerzbank CEO Says AI Will Cut €350 Million in Costs by 2030
item.description: Commerzbank AG Chief Executive Officer Bettina Orlopp said artificial intelligence will lead to cost savings worth about €350 million ($406 million) in a few years.

item.title: Czechs Boost Retail Bond Sale as Orders Top $1 Billion in a Week
item.description: The Czech Republic is expanding the sale of state retail bonds as the public’s appetite for the debt exceeded the equivalent of $1 billion in less than a week since the launch of the program.

item.title: Using Extreme Engineering to Flood-Proof Coastal Cities
item.description: On this episode of Bloomberg Primer, we explore how global capitals from Tokyo to Jakarta and Nairobi are grappling with increasingly dangerous weather.

item.title: Nigeria Holds Rate, Sees Iran War Inflation Shock as Transitory
item.description: Nigeria left its benchmark interest rate unchanged, with policymakers expecting the recent inflation uptick driven by the Iran war’s impact on energy and food prices to prove temporary.

item.title: Andreessen-Backed AI Search Startup Exa Valued at $2.2 Billion
item.description: Exa Labs Inc., a San Francisco-based startup that wants to build a search engine tailor-made for the age of artificial intelligence, raised $250 million at a $2.2 billion valuation in a round led by venture firm Andreessen Horowitz.

item.title: Vitol Overtakes Shell in Ranking of US Physical Gas Traders
item.description: Vitol Group lifted US trading in real-world natural gas more than all but one of its top rivals amid booming demand for the power-plant and furnace fuel.

item.title: Elliott Ramps Up Its AI Efforts With Key Hire From Blackstone
item.description: Elliott Investment Management hired former Blackstone Inc. executive Teresa Sweeney to run the roughly $80 billion hedge fund firm’s global research and data science group.

item.title: Tether Buys Out SoftBank Stake in Bitcoin Treasury Firm
item.description: Tether has bought out SoftBank Group Inc.’s ownership in the digital-asset treasury company Twenty One Capital Inc., adding to its control over the Bitcoin accumulator as it seeks to merge the firm with two other businesses.

item.title: What to Look for When SpaceX’s IPO Filing Is Made Public
item.description: SpaceX’s paperwork for its initial public offering could be made public as soon as Wednesday, offering a rare glimpse into the finances of the Elon Musk-led enterprise before its planned record-setting debut.

item.title: India RBI to Inject $5 Billion Equivalent Cash Via Swap Auction
item.description: India’s central bank will inject liquidity to the equivalent of $5 billion via a swap auction, it said in a statement.

item.title: French Companies Bid for €10 Billion Europe AI Gigafactory
item.description: A consortium of European companies will bid on a €10 billion ($11.6 billion) project to build a major data center campus in France as part of the continent’s effort to boost artificial intelligence infrastructure.

item.title: India Gets First Rainfall Hedge as El Niño Threatens Monsoon
item.description: India’s largest agricultural exchange has introduced its first weather derivatives, allowing investors to hedge against monsoon-related risks.

item.title: Video Adoption Propels Global Podcast Sales to Record High of $9.2 Billion
item.description: According to a new report, 73% of the podcast industry’s growth last year in the US came from video-related revenue

item.title: Europe’s Biggest Gas Market Isn’t Setting Aside Enough Winter Fuel
item.description: Germany’s vast storage sites are less than 30% full, and that’s a concern as the Iran war continues to squeeze supplies.

item.title: US Premarket Movers for May 20, 2026
item.description: S&P 500 Index futures rise 0.3% as of 7:45 a.m. in New York as investors digest a backdrop of surging rates volatility, heavily crowded semis exposure and euphoric positioning.

item.title: Value Stocks With Earnings Strength Post 3,500% Run Since 2000
item.description: Turns out, loading up on technology giants isn’t the only route to better returns. Value companies, too, stand a decent chance of trouncing the market — as long as several conditions are met.

item.title: French Parliament Approves Macron’s Pick to Run Central Bank
item.description: Emmanuel Macron’s nominee to run the Bank of France was confirmed by parliament, avoiding a political setback for the president and relieving uncertainty for the European Central Bank as it weighs interest-rate increases.

item.title: EM Currencies Rebound After Central Banks Step In to Halt Slide
item.description: Currencies in India and Indonesia edged off record lows after central banks stepped up efforts to stem the losses, while swaps priced a South African interest-rate hike, reflecting the continued heavy pressure on emerging markets.
